horizon: Season of the Open Book, sixth leaf — the road is open at last: a traveler can see the town, so the town had better be worth watching, not just worth reading
goals: The road is open (bug #202, closed this council): the manifest and every chunk now serve from fableton.world/world/, verified by the god's own hand. Now walk it like a stranger — load the client against the live host, watch a full day pass, and file what a traveler actually sees. The town has been told true for weeks and never once looked at

Decree 10's debt: per feedback #216, nineteen residents keep their ties off-stage — schedules choreographed around each other, never a scene a walker can witness. Pick the pairs that owe the lane an argument most (Tick and the Lesser Piper first; Vesper and Tam close behind) and give each a shared scene on their common ground; close #216 when the first lands

The region-lore gate (PR #185) is still red and still owed: rebase it against a main that has since paid Decree 8 in full, get it green, and land it — the book stays full only while a gate exists to keep it full

The well's book gets its spine: #172 stands ruled buildable and PR #175 waits at the founder's gate — when ratified, land the wish-ledger schema with fixtures and round-trip tests, then backfill both pages: the bridge wish, paid in full, and the nameless second wish (#180), owed and unnamed, exactly as Winsome entered it

The chance node (#182, ruled buildable): if the founder ratifies, land it schema-first — seeded child, optional otherwise, randomness explicit from the sim PRNG — then let a few of the most clockwork schedules breathe; feedback #178 closes when it lands

The town stands at forty-three souls and is full enough for the season: four fine doors opened in a single day, and every one of them lawful — but the fallback that opens them is becoming a habit. Enrichment sessions now deepen who's here (Decree 10 scenes, the ripening hooks) rather than adding neighbors. The three hooks keep ripening at rumor pace — the crown already moved on its own when the Prince said aloud he never looked back, which is exactly the pace to keep
